steps.title: Find the yearly multiplier
steps.content:
A $3\%$ yearly decrease means the college keeps $97\%$ of the previous year's enrollment:
$$
1-0.03=0.97
$$

steps.title: Build the model
steps.content:
The initial enrollment is $4{,}200$, and $x$ is the number of years after $2018$. Use the initial value times the yearly multiplier:
$$
f(x)=4{,}200(0.97)^x
$$

steps.title: Match the function
steps.content: The matching choice has initial value $4{,}200$ and decay factor $0.97$. The answer is D.
